violations.text: The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Service called during time of inspection. Establishment must use 3 bay sink to sanitize dishes. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2025

Vacuum packaged mahi mahi and tuna were observed in walk-in cooler thawing with the packaging intact at 36 and 29 degrees Fahrenheit. Items were removed from the packaging during time of inspection. Proper thawing methods reviewed during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2025

The presence of small flies is not controlled as evidenced by lice insects observed in upstairs bar and kitchen during time of inspection. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 09/01/2025

The receiving doors, located in the basement, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 09/01/2025
Condensate from upstairs walk-in cooler is draining into basin of 3 bay sink. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025

The following was observed in poor repair: -floors in upstairs walk-in cooler -floors in basement by ice machine -wall by icemachine The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

The fanguards in the upstairs walk-in cooler and wall behind dishmachine area have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 09/01/2025
